title: Swinney to 'explore' banning US flights from Prestwick

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: First Minister John Swinney speaking as he attends the opening of the Techscaler hub at Water's Edge, Dundee. (Image: Jane Barlow/PA Wire) John Swinney has said he would consider banning US military flights from using Prestwick if it emerged that the airport was being used to support offensive military operations in Iran. Speaking to the BBC’s Laura Kuenssberg , the First Minister said he was “seeking clarity from the United Kingdom Government”.

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: The Ministry of Defence has previously confirmed that American bombers had landed at RAF Fairford in Gloucestershire and that US forces had begun using British bases for what officials described as “specific defensive operations”. Lack of clarity from Washington over its Iran objectives threatens to reshape NATO Braveheart features in bizarre White House war film amid ongoing strikes on Iran Sir Keir Starmer initially refused permission for the US to use UK bases in the opening phase of the strikes against Iran but later agreed to allow defensive operations from British territory.

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: The United States and Israel launched strikes on Iran last week, targeting missile infrastructure, military sites and leadership figures.

sourceReferences.citationText: serper-scottish-aviation – Swinney considering ban on US military flights using Prestwick
